body.title: [sprint] Correct billing taxonomy to cost-rank + register Hex local tiers — local-first falls out of the ranking
body.intention: I intend that DickSimnel's builder inference runs on free local Hex, so the DS cost constraint lifts and the first real build is affordable.
body.description:
**Problem:** DS 'ollama-only mode' requests model `devstral-small-2:24b`, but EVERY Ollama ModelSpec in the registry is bound to source `ollama_cloud` — the local `ollama` source (sources.py:254, base_url from `INFERENCE_ENDPOINT`, default 127.0.0.1:11434) has ZERO models registered. So a worker dispatch resolves to the CLOUD, never to Hex. Pointing `INFERENCE_ENDPOINT` at Hex alone does nothing because the rules engine has no local-ollama candidate to pick.
**CONFIRMED HEX ROSTER (probed/pulled 2026-06-30, sized to 32GB unified memory):** minion=`llama3.2:3b` (2GB), worker=`devstral-small-2:24b` (15GB, agentic) + `qwen2.5-coder:14b` (9GB, coder fallback), analyst=`deepseek-r1:14b` (9GB, reasoner). designer stays Claude-direct (cloud, by design). The registry's cloud Ollama tags do NOT transfer locally: `qwen3-coder-next` is 48GB (won't fit) and `deepseek-v4-flash` 404s as a local tag — these stay on the `ollama_cloud` account (which is SUBSCRIPTION-metered, not flat-rate — see taxonomy fix below).
**ROOT CAUSE — billing labels are inverted/muddled (Akien 2026-06-30):** the engine sorts by a binary `billing_type` (flat_rate preferred over usage_based), and the labels are WRONG: local `ollama` (owned Hex hardware, ~free) is mislabeled `usage_based`, while `ollama_cloud` ($20/mo subscription, metered) is mislabeled `flat_rate`, and `google_free` ($0 but rate-limited) is also `flat_rate`. That inversion is exactly WHY cloud beats Hex. Fixing the taxonomy fixes the routing at the root — no 'local wins' special-case needed.
**COST-RANK LADDER (replaces the binary; lower = cheaper = preferred):** 0 graph-tree (compiled inference, no LLM call — reserve the rank) · 1 flat-rate / OWNED-local (Hex + igor cluster boxes 10.0.0.x; ~free at the margin) · 2 free-throttled (google_free — $0, rate-limited, external, unreliable) · 3 subscription-metered (ollama_cloud — fixed sub + usage caps) · 4 token-direct (openrouter / anthropic / paid-gemini — per-token). Rank-2-vs-3 is a judgment: $0 is cheaper than the sub, but throttled+external, so for must-complete work rank free-throttled BELOW subscription. 'Hex first' then falls out of the ladder with NO special-case.
**Proposed shape:** (1) **Correct the billing taxonomy — the root fix.** Replace the binary `billing_type` with a `cost_rank` per Source (sources.py) per the ladder above; fix the inverted labels (local ollama→1, ollama_cloud→3, google_free→2, openrouter/anthropic/paid-gemini→4). The candidate sort (rules_engine.py:200-219) keys on `cost_rank` ASC, priority as tiebreaker. REVISIT the `foreground` inversion (rules_engine.py:205-212): it currently flips flat_rate/usage_based and must be redefined against cost_rank (e.g. foreground = permit token-direct for latency). (2) Register a per-tier `ModelSpec(..., source_name='ollama', ...)` for each Hex model (minion=llama3.2:3b, worker=devstral-small-2:24b + qwen2.5-coder:14b, analyst=deepseek-r1:14b). route() pairs rule.model_id with rule.source_name INDEPENDENTLY of the spec's source (rules_engine.py:189-191) → no model_id collision; don't rename the cloud devstral spec. (3) Add per-tier `RoutingRule(task_class=<tier>, model=<hex-model>, source='ollama')`. (4) DS drops `model_override` and requests the `worker` TIER (tier-not-model contract — explicit model is the current violation). (5) Set `INFERENCE_ENDPOINT=http://10.0.0.100:11434` (local `ollama` source still points at 127.0.0.1 today). (6) Reconcile the DEAD source name `local_ollama` (rules_engine.py:95-96) → `ollama`.
**Escalation scope (OPEN — Akien to confirm):** this ticket covers (a) availability fallback — Hex DOWN → next cost-rank up — which is automatic once cost_rank sorts and `source.available` is honored. It does NOT cover (b) CAPABILITY escalation — Hex up but its model too weak → jump to the 48GB qwen3-coder-next on cloud — which needs a failure/quality TRIGGER + re-dispatch (a caller-side cascade like DS's _TIER_CASCADE), and should be its own ticket unless Akien folds it in.
**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/sources.py (billing_type → cost_rank per Source), models_registry.py (per-tier ollama ModelSpecs), rules_engine.py (sort on cost_rank; foreground redefine; local_ollama→ollama; per-tier ollama rules), devices/dicksimnel/device.py (drop model_override → worker tier); env (uu_bash_profile.sh / DS launch env). Inference Proxy only.
**Design rules:** ALL inference through the Proxy; no direct OR/Anthropic; log the source/model selection AND its cost_rank at the crossing; no SQLite.
**Scope boundary:** taxonomy + routing + registration + env. Does NOT touch ToolLoop, the trivial ticket, or the smoke driver (sibling tickets). Capability-escalation (b above) is OUT unless Akien folds it in. NOTE: cost_rank is CROSS-CUTTING (reorders ALL routing, every tier/caller), not just DS/worker — blast radius is the whole Proxy.
**Completion criteria:** (1) every Source carries a correct cost_rank (local ollama=1, google_free=2, ollama_cloud=3, openrouter/anthropic/paid-gemini=4; graph-tree reserved=0). (2) rules-engine unit test — a `worker` dispatch resolves to source `ollama` @ `http://10.0.0.100:11434` when local is available, and to the NEXT cost-rank up when Hex is unreachable; same for `minion` and `analyst`. (3) grep `local_ollama` → 0 live refs. (4) DS requests worker tier (no model_override). (5) existing routing for non-ollama tiers (designer, etc.) unchanged except where the corrected ranking intentionally reorders it.
**Test plan:** rules_engine selection test red→green per tier (cost_rank ASC picks Hex first; Hex-down → next rank, not hard-fail); a cost_rank ordering unit test (sorted sources match the ladder); foreground-mode test (token-direct permitted for latency under the new definition); grep-gate on `local_ollama`.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
